pattern: The probability of stopping at each total below the stopping threshold is a fixed fraction of a sliding window of recent probabilities, so a windowed dynamic program yields the chance of finishing within the limit.
hints: Define the probability of ever standing on each point total; draws stop once the total reaches the threshold.
Each reachable total below the threshold is entered from the previous maxPts totals, each with equal probability, which is a sliding-window average.
Maintain a running window sum of the last maxPts probabilities divided by maxPts; sum the probabilities of all stopping totals that are at most n to get the answer.

edge_inputs.description: one draw always lands at or below n
single draw, partial coverage of the limit
threshold zero means no draws, certain success
stopping totals can exceed n, less than certain
single point value makes the total deterministic
n far above the maximum reachable total

stress.description: random small parameters honoring k <= n and maxPts bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, max(1, size))
    k = rng.randint(0, n)
    maxPts = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    return [n, k, maxPts]
stress.note: keeps n modest so the O(n) dynamic program stays fast
